title: Roasted Artichokes
ingredients: Olive oil, 1 to 2 tablespoons per artichoke
Lemon slices, 1 thin slice per artichoke half
Garlic cloves, 1 peeled clove per artichoke half
Butter, about 1 teaspoon per artichoke half (optional)
Lemon juice, about 1 teaspoon per artichoke half (optional)
instructions: Choose artichokes that are 3 to 4 1/2 inches wide.
Break off and discard small outer leaves.
With a knife, slice off artichoke tips.
With scissors, snip thorn tips from remaining leaves.
Trim dark base from stem ends and peel coarse fibers from stems and artichoke bottoms with a knife.
Rinse artichokes well and drain briefly.
Choose a pan in which the artichokes fit close together in a single layer, leaving minimal large spaces to prevent juices from scorching.
Pour 1 to 2 tablespoons olive oil into the pan for each artichoke and add 1/4 teaspoon dried thyme.
Roll artichokes in oil to coat and turn cut sides down.
Slide 1 thin lemon slice and 1 peeled garlic clove under each artichoke half.
Bake in a 375° oven until artichoke bottoms are tender when pierced, 40 to 50 minutes.
Transfer artichokes, cut sides up, to a platter and top with lemon and garlic.

Sprinkle with salt to taste and drizzle with pan drippings, or prepare a sauce by adding butter and lemon juice to the roasting pan, heating over high heat (or in microwave at full power if container is microwave-safe) until butter melts, then drizzle over artichokes.
Serve hot or at room temperature.
For the dipping sauce, combine mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, soy sauce, and minced garlic in a bowl.
notes: 6 artichoke halves fit in a 9- by 13-inch pan
Artichokes can be served hot or at room temperature
The dipping sauce is served in a bowl for artichoke leaves
